defects.pr_title: [NPU] Fix warmup error with --disable-cuda-graph and mtp
defects.pr_body:
## Motivation
The server warmup encounters an error in forward_decode when --disable-cuda-graph is enabled. The root cause is the redundant padding tokens in the input tensor, which leads to a dimension mismatch for the NPU fused attention operator.
```
File "/xxx/sglang/srt/hardware_backend/npu/attention/ascend_backend.py", line 1846, in forward_decode
attn_output, _ = torch.ops.npu.npu_fused_infer_attention_score(
```

## Modification

Fix the padding logic in forward_decode for the NPU attention backend:

- Crop the query tensor to remove redundant padding tokens before calling the NPU fused attention operator
- Run the fused attention operator with the valid tensor
- Pad zeros back to restore the original tensor shape after the operator computation

## Accuracy Tests

```
export DEEP_NORMAL_MODE_USE_INT8_QUANT=0
export SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH=1

MODEL_PATH=/home/weights/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
python3 -m sglang.launch_server \
        --model-path ${MODEL_PATH} \
        --attention-backend ascend \
        --device npu \
        --tp-size 4 --nnodes 1 --node-rank 0 \
        --chunked-prefill-size -1 --max-prefill-tokens 16384 \
        --trust-remote-code \
        --host 127.0.0.1 --max-running-requests 128 \
        --mem-fraction-static 0.8 \
        --port 9903 \
        --mm-attention-backend ascend_attn --max-total-tokens 800000 \
        --dtype bfloat16 --mamba-ssm-dtype bfloat16 \
        --base-gpu-id 4 \
        --enable-multimodal \
        --disable-radix-cache \
        --dp-size 2 --enable-dp-attention --enable-dp-lm-head \
        --speculative-algorithm NEXTN --speculative-num-steps 3 --speculative-eagle-topk 1 --speculative-num-draft-tokens 4 \
        --speculative-draft-model-quantization unquant \
        --moe-a2a-backend deepep --deepep-mode auto \
        --disable-cuda-graph \
```

```
+-----------------+-----------+----------+------------------------------------------+-------+---------+----------------+
| Qwen3.6-35B-A3B | ceval     | mean_acc | OVERALL                                  |  1346 |  0.9005 | -              |
+-----------------+-----------+----------+------------------------------------------+-------+---------+----------------+

```

No impact.

reviews.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The logic to crop the query tensor `q` to match the batch size of `block_tables` is correct for fixing the MTP warmup mismatch where `num_tokens > batch_size`. However, for better consistency with the rest of the codebase and to avoid potential issues if `self.forward_metadata` is not fully initialized, it is recommended to use `forward_batch.batch_size` directly as the target batch size. Additionally, if the goal is to remove actual padding sequences (not just MTP tokens), `forward_batch.num_token_non_padded_cpu` should be used, but that would require slicing other inputs like `block_tables` and `actual_seq_len_kv` as well. Given the current implementation, using `forward_batch.batch_size` is a safer and cleaner alternative.

```suggestion
                num_token_padding = q.shape[0]
                actual_bs = forward_batch.batch_size
                q = q[:actual_bs]
```

defects.root_cause: Redundant padding tokens in the input tensor cause a dimension mismatch for the NPU fused attention operator during forward_decode with --disable-cuda-graph and MTP
defects.rationale: Warmup error during forward_decode with --disable-cuda-graph enabled. Root cause is redundant padding tokens not cropped before calling torch.ops.npu.npu_fused_infer_attention_score. Fix crops the query tensor to remove padding, runs the operator, then pads zeros back.

defects.pr_title: [NPU] [Bugfix] Wan quantization fix
defects.pr_body: ## Description Fix Wan2.2-T2V-A14B-Diffusers-w8a8/w8a8/mxfp8 quant scheme recognition on NPU by threading `reverse_param_names_mapping` through the ModelSlim config stack. ## Motivation Fix of https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/24518. After PR #23625 reshaped quantized‑diffusion prefixes, the Wan2.2‑W8A8 model stopped loading (`No modelslim compatible scheme`). The bug is that `_get_scheme_from_parts` uses the **model’s internal layer name** (e.g. `blocks.0.self_attn.to_q`) to look up the quant description, but the quant config file uses **architecture‑canonical names** (e.g. `blocks.0.attn1.to_q`). The WanVideo arch‑config already carries a `reverse_param_names_mapping` that translates internal names back to canonical names, but the ModelSlim initialisation path silently discarded this mapping. ## Modifications - **`modelslim.py`** – accept `reverse_param_names_mapping` in `__init__` and `from_config`, build a mapper via `get_param_names_mapping`, and use it in `_get_scheme_from_parts` before querying `quant_model_description.json`. - **`transformer_load_utils.py`** – read `reverse_param_names_mapping` from the arch config and pass it into `get_quant_config`. - **`quantization_utils.py`** – accept and forward `reverse_param_names_mapping`. - **`fsdp_load.py`** – add missing quant parameter suffixes (`input_offset`, `quant_bias`, `deq_scale`) to the sharding allow‑list; mark sharded tensors with `requires_grad=False` to avoid meta‑tensor copy errors. - **`npu/utils.py`** – update a performance warning to mention `--dit-cpu-offload false`. ## Accuracy Tests Command: ```SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_FN=2 SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_BN=1 SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_WARMUP=4 SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_RDT=0.4 SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_MC=4 SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_TAYLORSEER=true SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_TS_ORDER=2 SGLANG_CACHE_DIT_ENABLED=true sglang generate --model-path ./weights/Wan2.2-T2V-A14B-Diffusers-w8a8/ --prompt "Two anthropomorphic cats in comfy boxing gear and bright gloves fight intensely on a spotlighted stage." --height 720 --width 1280 --tp-size 2 --sp-degree 2 --num-gpus 4 --num-frames 81 --num-inference-steps 40 --warmup true``` https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/678e4823-0c70-47f5-82f5-e1f0e0f52a51 ## Speed Tests and Profiling ## Checklist - [x] Format your code according to the [Format code with pre-commit](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#format-code-with-pre-commit). - [ ] Add unit tests according to the [Run and add unit tests](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#run-and-add-unit-tests). - [ ] Update documentation according to [Write documentations](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#write-documentations). - [x] Provide accuracy and speed benchmark results according to [Test the accuracy](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#test-the-accuracy) and [Benchmark the speed](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#benchmark-the-speed). - [x] Follow the SGLang code style [guidance](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#code-style-guidance). ## Review and Merge Process 1. reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The fallback logic for `mapped_name` when `_name_mapper` is `None` is missing the `.weight` suffix. This will cause `quant_description.get(mapped_name)` to fail for standard layers that aren't mapped, as the keys in `quant_model_description.json` typically include the `.weight` suffix. Additionally, the logic for determining the `prefix` can be simplified by applying `removesuffix(".weight")` to the `mapped_name` in both cases.

```python
        full_weight_name = layer_name + ".weight"
        if self._name_mapper is not None:
            mapped_name, _, _ = self._name_mapper(full_weight_name)
        else:
            mapped_name = full_weight_name

        quant_type = self.quant_description.get(mapped_name, "")
        prefix = mapped_name.removesuffix(".weight")
```
maybe we should move `import` above `if`
Tried, not worked

defects.root_cause: reverse_param_names_mapping discarded in ModelSlim init path, causing _get_scheme_from_parts to use internal model layer names instead of architecture-canonical names for quant config lookup in Wan2.2-T2V-A14B-Diffusers W8A8/mxfp8
defects.rationale: Wan2.2 diffusion model fails to load with 'No modelslim compatible scheme' because reverse_param_names_mapping is not threaded through the ModelSlim config stack. After PR #23625 reshaped quantized-diffusion prefixes, the quant config file uses architecture-canonical names while the model uses internal layer names. Fix threads the mapping through modelslim.py, transformer_load_utils.py, quantization_utils.py, and fsdp_load.py.

defects.pr_title: [NPU] Fix mrope_position computation in Eagle Worker v2 with PlanStream
defects.pr_body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.io to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

<!-- Describe the purpose and goals of this pull request. -->
When SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2 and SGLANG_ENABLE_OVERLAP_PLAN_STREAM are enabled in multimodal scenarios, the Eagle Worker v2 computes mrope_position on the plan stream using draft outputs from the default stream. This race condition leads to incorrect mrope_position values.

defects.root_cause: Race condition: mrope_position is computed on the plan stream using draft outputs from the default stream when SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2 and SGLANG_ENABLE_OVERLAP_PLAN_STREAM are both enabled, producing incorrect positional values for Eagle speculative decoding in multimodal scenarios
defects.rationale: When both SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2 and SGLANG_ENABLE_OVERLAP_PLAN_STREAM are enabled in multimodal scenarios, Eagle Worker v2 computes mrope_position on the plan stream while draft outputs are on the default stream, causing a race condition that produces incorrect mrope_position values. Fix computes mrope_position on the default stream and copies to the graph buffer when CUDA graph is enabled.

reviews.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The causal mask creation can be simplified to a single operation using `~torch.triu`. This avoids redundant allocations and `masked_fill` calls, making the code more concise and slightly more efficient.

```python
        attn_mask = ~torch.triu(
            torch.ones(
                (seq_len, seq_len), device=hidden_states.device, dtype=torch.bool
            ),
            diagonal=1,
        )
```
Solved, torch.tril is better.

defects.root_cause: Attn_mask dtype mismatch (float vs expected bool/BFloat16) in NPU attention causing RuntimeError during SANA diffusion model execution, plus additional GPU-side Gemma2 encoder and DPM scheduler errors
defects.rationale: SANA diffusion model execution errors on NPU (RuntimeError: 'Expected attn_mask dtype to be bool or to match query dtype, but got attn_mask.dtype: float and query.dtype: c10::BFloat16') and GPU. Fixes span activation functions, Gemma2 encoder, and DPM multistep scheduler. PR body is truncated and provides limited detail on the exact fixes.

![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

Passing `experimental_config` as a keyword argument to `torch.profiler.profile` will cause a `TypeError` on non-NPU platforms (CUDA/CPU). This argument is specific to the `torch_npu` profiler extension. Since the profiler is only patched to support this argument when `_is_npu` is true (see lines 24-32), the standard PyTorch profiler used on other platforms will fail with an 'unexpected keyword argument' error.

You should use dictionary unpacking to conditionally pass this argument only when running on NPU. Additionally, please ensure this fix is also applied to `python/sglang/srt/utils/profile_utils.py` for consistency, as the V2 profiler implementation there currently lacks this configuration for NPU.

```python
                **({"experimental_config": torch_npu.profiler._ExperimentalConfig(
                    export_type=torch_npu.profiler.ExportType.Text,
                    profiler_level=torch_npu.profiler.ProfilerLevel.Level1,
                    msprof_tx=False,
                    aic_metrics=torch_npu.profiler.AiCMetrics.AiCoreNone,
                    l2_cache=False,
                    op_attr=False,
                    data_simplification=False,
                    record_op_args=False,
                    gc_detect_threshold=None,
                )} if _is_npu else {}),
```

The fix adds experimental_config for torch profiler on NPU to enable operator shape recording and operator statistics collection.

defects.pr_title: Fix diffusion fallback guards and validation
defects.pr_body:
## Summary
- Fix native diffusion RoPE fallbacks to accept full-width interleaved cos/sin caches, matching the Triton path.
- Tighten CuTe fused scale/shift validation for `[B, F, 1, D]` inputs where `S` is not divisible by `F`.
- Add a focused test for the frame divisibility guard.

## Validation
- `python3 -m py_compile python/sglang/jit_kernel/diffusion/triton/torch_fallback.py python/sglang/jit_kernel/diffusion/triton/npu_fallback.py python/sglang/jit_kernel/diffusion/cutedsl/scale_residual_norm_scale_shift.py`

## Note
- Dropped the diffusion case parser/dashboard changes from this PR so it no longer changes CI partition/coverage behavior. Parser append handling should be handled separately once the newly exposed dynamic cases are stable.
defects.root_cause: Native diffusion RoPE fallback (torch/npu path) rejects full-width interleaved cos/sin caches that the Triton path accepts, and CuTe fused scale/shift validation fails for inputs where S is not divisible by F
defects.rationale: Diffusion RoPE fallback path doesn't handle full-width interleaved cos/sin cache format, causing potentially wrong positional encoding when the Triton path is unavailable. Also tightens CuTe fused scale/shift validation to catch non-divisible (S,F) input shapes. Limited to edge cases in fallback paths and validation guards.

reviews.body: can you justify this change? the point of trunc_len is at lease to reserve one page for prefilling not only to align with page size. e.g., if rem_chunk_tokens == 2 * page_size, the trunc_len would leave no pages left

reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `forward_prepare_npu` method is missing the short-circuit logic for empty `hidden_states`. This is critical in distributed settings to prevent potential hangs when `o_proj.reduce_results` is enabled, as some ranks might skip the all-reduce while others wait. It also avoids unnecessary kernel calls with empty tensors. Additionally, the commented-out code should be removed for better code quality.

```python
        if hidden_states.shape[0] == 0:
            assert (
                not self.o_proj.reduce_results
            ), "short-circuiting allreduce will lead to hangs"
            return hidden_states, forward_batch, None

        qkv, _ = self.qkv_proj(hidden_states)
        if self.use_qk_norm:
            cos_sin = self.rotary_emb.cos_sin_cache.index_select(0, positions.flatten())
            cos, sin = cos_sin.chunk(2, dim=-1)
            q, k, v = split_qkv_tp_rmsnorm_rope(
                input=qkv,
                cos=cos,
                sin=sin,
                q_weight=self.q_norm.weight,
                k_weight=self.k_norm.weight,
                q_hidden_size=self.q_size,
                kv_hidden_size=self.kv_size,
                head_dim=self.head_dim,
                rotary_dim=self.rotary_dim,
                eps=self.q_norm.variance_epsilon,
                tp_world=self.q_norm.attn_tp_size,
                tp_group=get_attention_tp_group().device_group,
            )
        else:
            q, k, v = qkv.split([self.q_size, self.kv_size, self.kv_size], dim=-1)
            q, k = q.contiguous(), k.contiguous()
            q, k = self.rotary_emb(positions, q, k)

        inner_state = q, k, v, forward_batch
        return None, forward_batch, inner_state
```

defects.pr_title: [NPU] Fix DeepEP LL dispatch BF16 flag and skip triton kernel on NPU for Qwen3.5
defects.pr_body:
## Motivation
Fix two bugs that block NPU inference for Qwen3.5 MoE models with DeepEP backend.

## Modifications

### 1. DeepEP low-latency dispatch ignores `SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH` (`deepep.py`)
The normal dispatch path respects `SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH` to skip FP8 quantization, but the low-latency dispatch path (`_dispatch_core`) did not check this flag. It always quantized hidden states to INT8/FP8 during all-to-all communication, causing a dtype mismatch on NPU where `npu_grouped_matmul` does not support INT8 input with BF16 weights.

Fix : Added `and not envs.SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH.get()` to the `use_fp8` condition in `_dispatch_core` , consistent with the normal dispatch path.

### 2. Skip triton kernel on NPU in Qwen3.5 GDN forward (`qwen3_5.py`)
The `fused_qkvzba_split_reshape_cat_contiguous` triton kernel uses a 2D grid that crashes when token count exceeds 65536. This kernel is GPU-only and unnecessary on NPU.

Fix : Added `and not _is_npu` to the condition so NPU uses the PyTorch-native `fix_query_key_value_ordering` path instead.

## Accuracy Tests

```
export DEEP_NORMAL_MODE_USE_INT8_QUANT=0
export SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH=1

MODEL_PATH=/home/weights/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
python3 -m sglang.launch_server \
        --model-path ${MODEL_PATH} \
        --attention-backend ascend \
        --device npu \
        --tp-size 4 --nnodes 1 --node-rank 0 \
        --chunked-prefill-size -1 --max-prefill-tokens 16384 \
        --trust-remote-code \
        --host 127.0.0.1 --max-running-requests 128 \
        --mem-fraction-static 0.8 \
        --port 9903 \
        --mm-attention-backend ascend_attn --max-total-tokens 800000 \
        --dtype bfloat16 --mamba-ssm-dtype bfloat16 \
        --base-gpu-id 4 \
        --enable-multimodal \
        --disable-radix-cache \
        --dp-size 2 --enable-dp-attention --enable-dp-lm-head \
        --cuda-graph-bs 8 12 16 20 24 32 40 48 56 64 \
        --speculative-algorithm NEXTN --speculative-num-steps 3 --speculative-eagle-topk 1 --speculative-num-draft-tokens 4 \
        --speculative-draft-model-quantization unquant \
        --moe-a2a-backend deepep --deepep-mode auto \
```

```
+-----------------+-----------+----------+------------------------------------------+-------+---------+----------------+
| Qwen3.6-35B-A3B | ceval     | mean_acc | OVERALL                                  |  1346 |  0.893  | -              |
+-----------------+-----------+----------+------------------------------------------+-------+---------+----------------+
```

## Speed Tests and Profiling

No speed impact. The `fix_query_key_value_ordering` path on NPU uses basic PyTorch ops and has similar cost to the triton kernel.

## Checklist

- [x] Format your code according to the [Format code with pre-commit](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#format-code-with-pre-commit).

defects.pr_title: [diffusion] Fix --warmup-resolutions hang with --enable-cfg-parallel
defects.pr_body:
## Summary

Fixes a 30-minute silent hang when SGLang-diffusion is launched with `--enable-cfg-parallel --warmup --warmup-resolutions <list>`. The synthesized warmup `Req`s were built with `Req(prompt="", negative_prompt="", ...)`, which auto-created a base `SamplingParams` with `guidance_scale=1.0`. `Req.validate()` left `do_classifier_free_guidance=False`, so in CFG-parallel mode rank 1 skipped the positive pass, returned `None` for `noise_pred`, and `scheduler.step()` crashed with:

```
TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for *: 'Tensor' and 'NoneType'
  at scheduling_flow_match_euler_discrete.py:537
```

The exception was caught inside `GPUWorker`, `/health` kept returning 200, and the crash only surfaced as a 30-minute `gloo` broadcast timeout (`dist_timeout=3600`) when real requests arrived — turning a 7-second logic error into a half-hour silent hang.

## Root cause chain

1. `Scheduler.prepare_server_warmup_reqs` (`scheduler.py:238-258`) synthesizes `Req(prompt="", negative_prompt="")` with no `sampling_params` argument.
2. `Req.__init__` (`schedule_batch.py:169-182`) auto-creates a **base** `SamplingParams()` (not the model-specific subclass) on the first `setattr` of a SamplingParams field. Base defaults include `guidance_scale=1.0`.
3. `Req.validate` (`schedule_batch.py:270-285`) only sets `do_classifier_free_guidance=True` when `cfg_scale > 1.0 AND negative_prompt is not None`. With `cfg_scale=1.0`, the flag stays `False`.
4. `_predict_noise_with_cfg` (`denoising.py:1596-1621`) returns `noise_pred_cond=None` on `cfg_rank != 0` when `do_cfg=False`.
5. `scheduler.step(model_output=None)` crashes at `prev_sample = sample + dt * model_output` in `scheduling_flow_match_euler_discrete.py:537`.

## Repro

```bash
sglang serve --model-path Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512 --num-gpus 2 \
    --enable-cfg-parallel --warmup --warmup-resolutions 512x512 1024x1024 \
    --enable-torch-compile --trust-remote-code
# Hangs ~30 min, then scheduler terminates with:
#   Error receiving requests in scheduler event loop (attempt 1/3):
#   [/pytorch/third_party/gloo/gloo/transport/tcp/unbound_buffer.cc:78]
#   Timed out waiting 1800000ms for recv operation to complete
```

## Before / After

| Scenario | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Warmup with `--warmup-resolutions --enable-cfg-parallel` | 30-min gloo timeout + rank-1 death | Works |
| Real request without CFG to cfg-parallel server | 30-min gloo timeout + rank-1 death | Instant `ValueError` |
| Real request with CFG (cfg-parallel on or off) | Works | Works (unchanged) |
| Warmup without `--enable-cfg-parallel` (image or text) | Works | Works (byte-identical `Req` kwargs) |

## Commits

1. **`[diffusion] scheduler: fix warmup Req synthesis under --enable-cfg-parallel`**
   `prepare_server_warmup_reqs` now builds the warmup `Req` via conditional kwargs. When `enable_cfg_parallel` is on, it sets `do_classifier_free_guidance=True` and `negative_prompt="warmup"` so rank 1 produces a valid `noise_pred_uncond` and `_combine_cfg_parallel`'s all-reduce succeeds. When cfg-parallel is off, the Req construction is byte-identical to the pre-fix behavior.

2. **`[diffusion] stages: reject non-CFG requests under --enable-cfg-parallel`**
   `InputValidationStage.forward` raises `ValueError` when the server was launched with `--enable-cfg-parallel` but the request has `do_classifier_free_guidance=False`. The error message distinguishes `negative_prompt` states (`not set` / `empty` / `set`) and notes that `negative_prompt_embeds` is also a valid way to enable CFG. Follows the existing per-request validation pattern (`input_validation.py:295-298`, `311-314`) and the server-args `_validate_cfg_parallel` at `server_args.py:1340-1343`. This is a defensive guard — not strictly required to fix the bug, but preempts the 30-min hang on any future misconfig or regression of commit 1.

3. **`[diffusion] test: add unit tests for --enable-cfg-parallel warmup fix`**
   Four CPU-only unit tests in `test/unit/test_cfg_parallel_warmup.py` covering both commits (positive + regression for the scheduler fix; positive + negative for the input-validation guard).

## Test plan

- [x] New unit tests pass locally (`pytest python/sglang/multimodal_gen/test/unit/test_cfg_parallel_warmup.py` → 4/4 green).
- [x] Existing `test/unit/` suite still passes.
- [x] Full `pytest python/sglang/multimodal_gen/test/` (excluding `server/` and `cli/`) → 154/155 passed, 1 pre-existing skip.
- [x] Integration validation: reran the failing Qwen-Image-2512 resolution sweep on 2x B200 with `--warmup-resolutions 512x512 1024x1024 --enable-cfg-parallel`. All cfg-parallel steps finish cleanly (previously all hung for 30 min).

## Follow-ups (not in this PR)

- The 30-min `gloo` broadcast timeout masks rank death — `/health` should reflect worker-process state, or the scheduler's recv loop should notice rank-1 termination faster.
- `Req.__init__` auto-creates a base `SamplingParams()` instead of the model-specific subclass when a calle

reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

`size` is a `SizeDict` (which is a `TypedDict` in the transformers library) and should be accessed using bracket notation (e.g., `size["shortest_edge"]`). Additionally, since `size` is marked as `Optional` in the function signature, you should handle the case where it is `None` by falling back to `self.size` to avoid a runtime `AttributeError`.

```suggestion
                    min_pixels=size["shortest_edge"] if size is not None else self.size["shortest_edge"],
                    max_pixels=size["longest_edge"] if size is not None else self.size["longest_edge"],
```

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The permutation order `(0, 1, 4, 3, 2, 5, 6)` swaps the `channel` (dim 3) and `temporal_patch_size` (dim 2) dimensions. According to the standard Qwen2-VL/Qwen3-VL implementation, the flattened feature vector should have `temporal_patch_size` before `channel`. This swap will lead to incorrect model inputs for videos where `temporal_patch_size > 1` (unlike images where it is typically 1).

```suggestion
            patches = patches.permute(0, 1, 4, 2, 3, 5, 6)
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

For consistency with the image preprocessing logic in this file (line 72) and to avoid the overhead of an extra function call, consider accessing the shape directly using `stacked_videos.shape[-2:]`.

```suggestion
            resized_height, resized_width = stacked_videos.shape[-2:]
```

defects.rationale: Two NPU-specific bugfixes for diffusion models: (1) NZ format conversion for CPU-offloaded tensors is unsupported on NPU — changed from attempted conversion to warning message since the workaround is non-functional; (2) torch.npu.config.allow_internal_format=True is incorrectly enabled for non-quantized models, reducing performance even without NZ conversion calls (not caught by CI since warmup masks the issue). Both changes restore expected diffusion performance on NPU.

defects.pr_body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.io to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

The previous PR [17511](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/pull/17511)  added a condition ( forward_mode.is_extend() ) to avoid using fused Triton kernel (split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope) during prefill stage. However, when using speculative inference (such as eagle3)，the condition will become invalid because the decode stage will be TARGET_VERIFY, and forward_mode.is_extend() will always be true. This will make the fusion kernel  unusable the decode phase.

## Modifications

Change the condition from  forward_mode.is_extend()  to forward_mode.is_extend_or_draft_extend_or_mixed().

defects.root_cause: forward_mode.is_extend() condition blocks fused split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope kernel during speculative decode because TARGET_VERIFY forward mode also returns is_extend()=True, making condition always-true during decode
defects.rationale: PR #17511 added forward_mode.is_extend() guard to avoid using fused triton split_qkv_rmsnorm_rope kernel during prefill stage. However, during speculative inference (eagle3), the decode stage uses TARGET_VERIFY forward mode where is_extend() returns True, causing the condition to always evaluate as True and permanently disabling the fusion kernel during decode. Fix changes condition to forward_mode.is_extend_or_draft_extend_or_mixed() to correctly identify only prefill/extend stages.

```python
        max_num_blocks = (total_context_len + self.page_size - 1) // self.page_size
```

reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The path to the BiSheng compiler changed in CANN 8.5, but hardcoding the new path breaks backward compatibility for users on older CANN versions (e.g., 6.x or 7.x) where the path was `compiler/ccec_compiler/bin/bisheng`. 

Additionally, if the compiler is not found at the specified path, `subprocess.check_output` will raise a `FileNotFoundError`. Since `FileNotFoundError` is a subclass of `OSError` and not `subprocess.SubprocessError`, it will not be caught by the `except` block on line 370, causing the script to crash. 

It is recommended to check multiple candidate paths and ensure that a caught exception is raised if the compiler is missing.

```suggestion
            for path in ["tools/bisheng_compiler/bin/bisheng", "compiler/ccec_compiler/bin/bisheng"]:
                bisheng = os.path.join(CANN_HOME, path)
                if os.path.isfile(bisheng):
                    break
            else:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(1, "bisheng")
```
there is syntax error here, `if not os.path.isfile(bisheng):`
maybe we can use `pathlib.Path(CANN_HOME).rglob("bisheng")` to avoid hardcoding
thanks, I fixed it and forgot to push

I thought about it, but finally I think it is better to hardcode. Because it is possible to find several bisheng files and user can copy it to another directory. So we should take exact from this path.

defects.pr_title: [Fix] eagle/eagle3 speculative decoding conflicts with xgrammar in NPU
defects.pr_body: ## Motivation Fix when turn on the eagle/eagle3 spec-decoding and send a request with json_schema, as reported in [#20148](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/20148). The reason is that , on Ascend NPU, apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton can fail to enforce grammar masks for XGrammar-constrained decoding: tokens that are masked out (mask_allows=False) may still keep high logits and be selected, causing downstream Tokens not accepted errors in grammar.accept_token. This PR supersedes a previously closed PR (#20168), which was accidentally invalidated due to a force push. This version reintroduces the changes on a clean branch. ## Modifications This PR adds an NPU-safe fallback for constrained mask application: For logits.device.type == "npu" in XGrammar, use a CPU-based bitmask unpack + NPU masked_fill(-inf) path. Keep existing Triton/CUDA paths unchanged for non-NPU devices. Move the NPU fallback helper into triton_ops/bitmask_ops.py for centralized mask-op management. ## Accuracy Tests server responses correctly and the response keeps consistent with the case where eagle is turned off. ## Benchmarking and Profiling ## Checklist - [x] Format your code according to the [Format code with pre-commit](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#format-code-with-pre-commit). - [x] Add unit tests according to the [Run and add unit tests](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#run-and-add-unit-tests). - [x] Update documentation according to [Write documentations](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#write-documentations). - [x] Provide accuracy and speed benchmark results according to [Test the accuracy](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#test-the-accuracy) and [Benchmark the speed](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#benchmark-the-speed). - [ ] Follow the SGLang code style [guidance](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#code-style-guidance). ## Review Process 1. defects.pr_title: [Bugfix] fix npu get kv_item_lens in PD separation when use ASCEND_US…
defects.pr_body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.io to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

<!-- Describe the purpose and goals of this pull request. -->
Original `k_buffer` shape is` (layer_num, size // page_size + 1, page_size, head_num, head_dim)`,
when enable `ASCEND_USE_FIA` `k_buffer `shape is `(layer_num, (size // page_size + 1) * page_size, 1, head_num, head_dim)`.

The element size of `self.get_key_buffer(i)[0]` in `def get_contiguous_buf_infos()`:
original is `page_size * head_num * head_dim`,
when enable `ASCEND_USE_FIA` is `1 * head_num * head_dim`.

So when enable `ASCEND_USE_FIA`, The calculation of kv_item_lens requires the addition of `* page_size`.

## Modifications

Fix kv_item_lens for FIA, in order to compute data size in PD transfer cases.

## Accuracy Tests

```
python3 few_shot_gms8k.py --data-path "/PATH/TO/test.jsonl.txt" --parallel 32 --num-questions 200

Invalid: 03000
Latency: 37.457
```

defects.pr_title: [Fix] Patch is_flash_attn_2_available for flash-attn-4 in VLM input format test
defects.pr_body:
## Motivation

sglang now depends on `flash-attn-4` (>= 4.0.0b4) introduced in https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/pull/20303 . Unlike `flash-attn` v2, `flash-attn-4` registers a bare `flash_attn` **namespace package** with no actual content — no `flash_attn_func`, no `flash_attn_varlen_func`, and no `flash_attn.bert_padding` submodule.

This causes `transformers.utils.is_flash_attn_2_available()` to return `True` (it can `find_spec("flash_attn")`), so HuggingFace remote model code that guards flash-attn v2 imports behind that check still attempts the import and crashes:

```
ImportError: cannot import name 'flash_attn_func' from 'flash_attn' (unknown location)
```

This breaks `TestKimiVLImageUnderstandsImage` in `test_vlm_input_format.py`, because the Kimi-VL remote `modeling_kimi_vl.py` does:

```python
if is_flash_attn_2_available():
    from flash_attn import flash_attn_func, flash_attn_varlen_func
    from flash_attn.bert_padding import index_first_axis, pad_input, unpad_input
```

at module level during `get_class_from_dynamic_module()`.

## Fix

Patch `is_flash_attn_2_available` to return `False` at both `transformers.utils.import_utils` and `transformers.utils` (since different code paths import from either location) before any HF dynamic module loading happens. The test only exercises the vision tower and does not need flash attention.

## Test plan
- [x] Verify `TestKimiVLImageUnderstandsImage` passes on CI with `flash-attn-4` installed

reviews.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This patch modifies global state by reassigning functions in `transformers.utils` and `transformers.utils.import_utils`. While this is a valid workaround for the described compatibility issue in a test environment, modifying global state can lead to unintended side effects in larger test suites or shared environments if not properly isolated or reverted.

Consider explicitly saving the original functions and restoring them in the `tearDownClass` method of the test class (`VLMInputTestBase` or `TestKimiVLImageUnderstandsImage`) to ensure this patch is localized to the scope of this test. This would prevent potential interference with other tests or modules that might rely on the original `is_flash_attn_2_available` behavior.

defects.root_cause: flash-attn-4 namespace package registers as importable flash_attn with no actual content, causing transformers.utils.is_flash_attn_2_available() to return True and HF remote model code to attempt importing nonexistent symbols (flash_attn_func, flash_attn_varlen_func) from empty namespace, causing ImportError on VLM model loading
defects.rationale: flash-attn-4 namespace package causes is_flash_attn_2_available() false positive. Calling non-existent flash attention functions would crash. Package version compatibility issue with no clear NPU-specific root cause.

defects.pr_title: [Fix] Slice input_embeds to extend_input_len in prepare_for_extend
defects.pr_body:
## Motivation

When `PrefillAdder` truncates a request on chunk overflow (`rem_chunk_tokens` exceeded), it adjusts `fill_ids` and `extend_input_len` but **not** `req.input_embeds`. `prepare_for_extend` then appends the full-length embedding array while `out_cache_loc` is sized for the truncated `extend_input_len`, causing:

```
RuntimeError: shape mismatch: value tensor of shape [N, ...]
  cannot be broadcast to indexing result of shape [M, ...]
```

in `set_kv_buffer`, with **N > M** (opposite polarity to the retraction bug in #14110, which is N < M — that's a different bug, same crash signature).

**Trigger condition:** many input_embeds requests arrive in one scheduler iteration (thundering herd) and their total tokens exceed `chunked_prefill_size`. In our RL setup this happens at rollout start immediately after `update_weights_from_distributed` — 256 requests land near-simultaneously. With 125-token prompts and `chunked_prefill_size=8192`: 65 requests fit (8125 tokens), request #66 gets truncated to 67 tokens but its 125-token embedding array flows through unsliced.

The `input_ids` path already handles this correctly since `fill_ids` itself is truncated by the adder.

## Modifications

Slice `req.input_embeds` with `[pre_len : pre_len + req.extend_input_len]` to match what was already applied to `fill_ids`. No-op for non-chunked requests (`pre_len=0`, `extend_input_len=len(embeds)`).

Chunk continuation works because `ChunkCache.cache_unfinished_req` sets `prefix_indices` to the processed length after each chunk, and `req.input_embeds` is never mutated — so chunk 2 correctly slices `[trunc_len : trunc_len + remaining]`.

## Accuracy Tests

This is a runtime crash fix. Verified on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct with `--disable-radix-cache --chunked-prefill-size 8192` under concurrent input_embeds load — previously crashed reliably at the chunking boundary, now completes cleanly.

No new unit test — the trigger requires concurrent load exceeding `chunked_prefill_size`, hard to repro in the serial `test_input_embeddings.py`. Same precedent as #17850 (merged chunked-prefill race fix without new test).

## Benchmarking and Profiling

N/A — stability fix, no hot-path changes. Slice is a no-op for the common (non-chunked) case.

## Checklist

- [x] Format your code according to the [Code Formatting with Pre-Commit](https://docs.sglang.ai/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#code-formatting-with-pre-commit)
- [ ] Add unit tests — manual repro verification (see above)
- [ ] Update documentation as needed — N/A

---

**Note:** this PR will have a trivial merge conflict with #20205 (same lines, `.extend()` → `.append()`). Correct resolution: `.append(req.input_embeds[pre_len : pre_len + req.extend_input_len])`.

reviews.body: do not need to register to AMD. This is general feature / test coverage.

defects.root_cause: PrefillAdder chunk truncation in scheduler adjusts fill_ids and extend_input_len but not req.input_embeds when chunked_prefill_size is exceeded on concurrent input_embeds requests, causing shape mismatch RuntimeError in set_kv_buffer because out_cache_loc is sized for truncated length but input_embeds is full length
defects.rationale: PrefillAdder truncation adjusts fill_ids and extend_input_len but not req.input_embeds, causing shape mismatch crash in set_kv_buffer. Input tensor slicing not propagated to embeddings.

defects.pr_body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.ai to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

<!-- Describe the purpose and goals of this pull request. -->
1. Ray in RL framework like verl will control environment variable such as ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES, which sometimes will cause original is_npu() function return False in sglang. Then code will go to cuda branch again
## Modifications
1. Remind user in case of hasattr(torch, "npu") =True and torch.npu.is_available()=False.

reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

This block of code duplicates the logic from the `is_npu()` function. This is a maintainability concern, as any future changes to the NPU detection logic or error message would need to be made in two places.

To avoid this, you can refactor this to call `is_npu()` directly:
```python
if is_npu():
    if device_id is None:
        return "npu"
    return f"npu:{device_id}"
```
This makes the code cleaner, removes the duplication, and ensures consistent behavior. Note that `is_npu()` already handles raising the `RuntimeError` if an NPU is detected but not available.

Additionally, it's more idiomatic to use `is None` for `None` checks and f-strings for formatting.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This logic can be simplified to reduce nesting, which improves readability. You can use a guard clause to check for the `torch.npu` attribute and return early if it's not present.

Consider this alternative structure:
```python
if not hasattr(torch, "npu"):
    return False

if not torch.npu.is_available():
    raise RuntimeError("torch_npu detected, but NPU device is not available or visible.")

return True
```
This flattened structure is often easier to follow.
our code style is GPU first, and i don't understand the root cause in this place, please give a more detailed report

This pr has fixed the wrong return https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/pull/13359/files#diff-1f1b431bcaea00cda2a62efe5d5f702f8bc0445982c1ec0b44d31d025f8faf68R62-R78
Now only remind user in case of hasattr(torch, "npu") =True and torch.npu.is_available()=False.

defects.root_cause: NPU device detection (is_npu()) returns False when RL frameworks such as Ray/verl control ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES environment variable
defects.rationale: When RL frameworks like Ray/verl control the ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES environment variable, the is_npu() function can incorrectly return False even though NPU devices are available. This causes SGLang to take the CUDA code path, leading to crashes on NPU hardware. The fix adds a user-facing warning when hasattr(torch, 'npu') is True but torch.npu.is_available() returns False.

defects.pr_title: [NPU]Fix for ipc handle with npu
defects.pr_body:
coauthor @lbk-sys 
## Motivation
Currently, NPU does not support UUID.  This will cause RuntimeError: Blocked unsafe class loading (torch_npu.multiprocessing.reductions.rebuild_npu_tensor)

## Modifications

<!-- Detail the changes made in this pull request. -->

reviews.body:
![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

The functions `_rebuild_npu_tensor_modified` and `npu_verl_to_sglang` are defined at the top level of the module, but they depend on `npu_reductions`, which is imported conditionally only when `_is_npu` is true. This will cause a `NameError` when this module is imported on a non-NPU platform, breaking execution.

To fix this, these NPU-specific functions should be defined inside the `if _is_npu:` block, along with the conditional import. I've also slightly improved the assertion message for better debuggability.

```python
if _is_npu:
    from torch_npu.multiprocessing import reductions as npu_reductions

    def _rebuild_npu_tensor_modified(*args):
        args = _modify_tuple(args, _REDUCE_TENSOR_ARG_DEVICE_INDEX, npu_verl_to_sglang)
        return npu_reductions._rebuild_npu_tensor_original(*args)

    def npu_verl_to_sglang(device: int):
        assert SGLANG_TP_RANK is not None, "SGLANG_TP_RANK is not registered. Please call register_sgl_tp_rank() first."
        return SGLANG_TP_RANK

SGLANG_TP_RANK = None

def monkey_patch_torch_reductions():
    """Monkey patching before Torch https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/149248 is fixed"""

    # Currently, NPU does not support UUID. This has been temporarily commented out, with support expected in the fourth quarter.
    if _is_npu:
        if hasattr(npu_reductions, "_rebuild_npu_tensor_original"):
            return
        npu_reductions._rebuild_npu_tensor_original = npu_reductions.rebuild_npu_tensor
        npu_reductions.rebuild_npu_tensor = _rebuild_npu_tensor_modified
    else:
        if hasattr(reductions, "_reduce_tensor_original"):
            return

        reductions._reduce_tensor_original = reductions.reduce_tensor
        reductions._rebuild_cuda_tensor_original = reductions.rebuild_cuda_tensor

        reductions.reduce_tensor = _reduce_tensor_modified
        reductions.rebuild_cuda_tensor = _rebuild_cuda_tensor_modified

        reductions.init_reductions()


# The signature has not been changed for years, and we will not need this when the next version is released,
# so it looks safe to use a constant.
_REDUCE_TENSOR_ARG_DEVICE_INDEX = 6


def register_sgl_tp_rank(rank: int):
    global SGLANG_TP_RANK
    SGLANG_TP_RANK = rank



```

Thank you. Already delete

defects.root_cause: NPU does not support UUID for IPC tensor handles, causing RuntimeError in torch_npu multiprocessing reductions
defects.rationale: NPU's torch_npu does not support UUID for IPC tensor reconstruction, unlike CUDA. This causes RuntimeError 'Blocked unsafe class loading (torch_npu.multiprocessing.reductions.rebuild_npu_tensor)' during multiprocessing. The fix addresses IPC handle serialization for NPU in model_runner.py and patch_torch.py.

reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

There's a misleading comment and an opportunity to refactor this block for better clarity and maintainability.

1.  **Incorrect Comment**: The comment on line 65 states `(chunked_prefill_size 8k, cuda_graph_max_bs 256 if tp < 4 else 64)`, but the code implements the opposite logic (`64 if tp < 4 else 256`). The implementation seems correct based on the pattern that a larger tensor parallelism size allows for a larger batch size. The comment should be corrected to match the code.

2.  **Refactoring**: The logic for setting `chunked_prefill_size` and `cuda_graph_max_bs` is duplicated. You can refactor this by first determining the configuration values and then applying them, which makes the code cleaner.

Here is a suggested implementation that addresses both points:

```python
    # NPU memory settings
    npu_mem = get_npu_memory_capacity()

    chunked_prefill_size = None
    cuda_graph_max_bs = None

    if npu_mem < 32 * 1024:
        # Ascend 910B4,910B4_1
        # (chunked_prefill_size 4k, cuda_graph_max_bs 16 if tp < 4 else 64)
        chunked_prefill_size = 4 * 1024
        cuda_graph_max_bs = 16 if args.tp_size < 4 else 64
    elif npu_mem < 64 * 1024:
        # Ascend 910B1,910B2,910B2C,910B3,910_9391,910_9392,910_9381,910_9382,910_9372,910_9362
        # (chunked_prefill_size 8k, cuda_graph_max_bs 64 if tp < 4 else 256)
        chunked_prefill_size = 8 * 1024
        cuda_graph_max_bs = 64 if args.tp_size < 4 else 256

    if args.chunked_prefill_size is None and chunked_prefill_size is not None:
        args.chunked_prefill_size = chunked_prefill_size
    if args.cuda_graph_max_bs is None and cuda_graph_max_bs is not None:
        args.cuda_graph_max_bs = cuda_graph_max_bs
```

defects.root_cause: NPU memory default settings processed through generic GPU memory settings handler causing incorrect branch selection (npu_memory_settings_branching)
defects.rationale: NPU memory default settings were processed in the generic _handle_gpu_memory_settings chain, which could cause issues when memory configuration does not fall into the expected branch. The fix extracts NPU-specific memory settings into a dedicated set_default_server_args function in npu/utils.py that precisely controls based on available NPU memory, and processes hardware-specific backend settings before the general handler.

defects.pr_title: [BugFix] fix prefixcache performance and accuracy on ascend
defects.pr_body: ## Motivation We have fixed some performance and accuracy problems in the MLA model on Ascend. ## Modifications For performance problems: 1. We moved **device_indices** to the CPU in advance in *cache_controller.py*. 2. In *memory_pool_host.py*, we added a new variable **NEED_HOST_REGISTER** and **self.host_register** to indicate whether **cudaHostRegister** is required for pinning memory. If it is not needed, tensors are allocated directly with **pin_memory=True**. For accuracy problems: - We integrated the **npu_ring_mla** operator to perform segmented computation for the attention module. ## Accuracy Tests We added a new test case, *test_ascend_hicache_mla.py*, to ensure the accuracy of the MLA model. The testing results are consistent with those obtained when the feature is disabled, indicating that the accuracy is normal. Test case results: ## Benchmarking and Profiling We tested the performance of the DeepSeek v3 model by comparing disabling the radix cache versus enabling L1 and L2 caches. The dataset used was 3.5K GSM8K with a 50% reuse rate (half of the requests are repeated), which ultimately **reduced TTFT by 39.5%**. Below are the specific execution and benchmark commands. ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Using `os.environ` directly is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase and bypasses the `envs` module. It is highly recommended to use `envs` to manage environment variables safely.

Additionally, if an exception is raised during the forward pass, these environment variables will not be restored, leading to state leakage that can affect subsequent requests or other models. Consider wrapping the forward pass in a `try...finally` block to guarantee that the environment variables are always restored:

```python
try:
    # forward pass logic
finally:
    if _is_npu and self.quant_config is None:
        envs.SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH.set(False)
        envs.DEEP_NORMAL_MODE_USE_INT8_QUANT.set(True)
```

```suggestion
            envs.DEEP_NORMAL_MODE_USE_INT8_QUANT.set(False)
```

If an exception is raised during the forward pass (e.g., in `self.model(...)` or `self.logits_processor(...)`), `exit_stack.close()` will not be executed. This will leak the overridden environment variables, potentially corrupting the state for subsequent requests or other models running in the same process.

To ensure exception safety, wrap the execution of the forward pass in a `try...finally` block:

```python

    def forward(
        self,
        input_ids: torch.Tensor,
        positions: torch.Tensor,
        forward_batch: ForwardBatch,
        input_embeds: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
        **kwargs,
    ):
        exit_stack = ExitStack()
        if (
            is_npu()
            and self.quant_config is None
            and get_global_server_args().quantization is not None
        ):
            # ascend mtp unquant
            exit_stack.enter_context(envs.SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH.override(True))
            exit_stack.enter_context(
                envs.DEEP_NORMAL_MODE_USE_INT8_QUANT.override(False)
            )

        try:
            assert input_embeds is None
            # ... rest of the forward pass logic ...
        finally:
            exit_stack.close()

        return self.logits_processor(
            input_ids, hidden_states, self.lm_head, forward_batch
        )
```

defects.pr_title: [Bugfix] Fix Ascend NPU CP attention for batch size > 1
defects.pr_body:
## Motivation

#23269 generalized attention context-parallel (CP) prefill from `bs == 1` to `bs > 1` by reshaping `ContextParallelMetadata` from single scalars into per-sequence lists/tensors. That change updated the CUDA FlashAttention path and the DSA indexer, but **missed the Ascend NPU FIA CP path** in `do_cp_attn_fia`. As a result, on NPU the CP-extend (prefill) attention now:

1. **Raises `AttributeError`** — it still reads the removed scalar fields `cp_meta.actual_seq_q_prev / actual_seq_q_next / kv_len_prev / kv_len_next`, which no longer exist on the new `ContextParallelMetadata` (only `*_list` / `*_tensor` / `total_q_prev_tokens` remain). This breaks even `bs == 1` CP on NPU on current main.
2. **Splits Q incorrectly for `bs > 1`** — it uses `torch.chunk(q, 2)` (global midpoint), but under the zigzag layout the local tokens are `[all_seqs_prev_blocks | all_seqs_next_blocks]`, so the split point is `total_q_prev_tokens`, not `len(q) // 2`.

This PR restores the Ascend NPU CP attention path so Qwen3-MoE (and other non-MLA GQA models on the FIA path) work end-to-end with `--enable-prefill-context-parallel` for `bs > 1`, matching the community bs>1 design from #23269.

Affected path is reached only when `ASCEND_USE_FIA=1`, the attention backend is `ascend`, and a pure CP-extend prefill batch is scheduled. MLA and the DSA/NSA path (`do_cp_balance_attn`) are out of scope (the DSA CP path remains bs=1 in #23269) and are not touched.

## Modifications

`python/sglang/srt/hardware_backend/npu/attention/ascend_backend.py`, method `do_cp_attn_fia`:

- Split Q at `cp_meta.total_q_prev_tokens` (`q[:split]` / `q[split:]`) instead of `torch.chunk(q, 2)`, so the prev/next halves match the per-sequence zigzag layout for `bs > 1`. (`torch.chunk` happened to be correct only for `bs == 1`.)
- Replace the removed scalar metadata fields in the two `npu_fused_infer_attention_score` calls with the per-sequence equivalents, aligned with the existing non-CP FIA convention in this file:
  - `actual_seq_lengths = np.cumsum(cp_meta.actual_seq_q_prev_list)` (cumulative q lengths), `actual_seq_lengths_kv = cp_meta.kv_len_prev_list` (per-seq kv lengths); analogous for the `_next` half.
- Fully backward compatible with `bs == 1`: `np.cumsum([x]) == [x]` and `total_q_prev_tokens == actual_seq_q_prev` in the single-sequence case.

## Accuracy Tests

Planned: GSM8K on Ascend 910C (A3), Qwen3-30B-A3B, `TP=4 / MOE_DP=2 / ATTN_CP=2`, `--enable-prefill-context-parallel` with `ASCEND_USE_FIA=1` (mirrors the registered test `test/registered/ascend/llm_models/test_npu_qwen3_30b_attn_cp.py`, target accuracy >= 0.92), compared against the TP-only / `bs == 1` baseline.

```plaintext
python test_gsm8k.py 
100%|█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| 300/300 [01:46<00:00,  2.82it/s]
Questions:        300
Accuracy:         0.9367  (281/300)
Invalid parses:   0.0000
Latency:          106.52 s
Output tokens:    35844
Output throughput: 336.51 tok/s
```

reviews.body:
![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The current implementation of `_collect_mamba_init_info` introduces multiple GPU-to-CPU synchronizations which can negatively impact scheduler performance, especially under high load. Specifically, `all_pool_idx.tolist()` (line 2087) and `req.mamba_cow_src_index.item()` (line 2094) are blocking operations that wait for the GPU. 

Since `req.mamba_pool_idx` and `req.mamba_cow_src_index` are already tensors on the device, you can perform these operations entirely on the GPU by collecting the tensors and using advanced indexing. This avoids moving data back and forth and eliminates the sync points.

```python
    def _collect_mamba_init_info(self, reqs):
        """Collect mamba cache indices and deferred COW/clear info from requests."""
        # Stack all pool indices on device to avoid sync
        all_pool_idx = torch.stack([req.mamba_pool_idx for req in reqs]).to(dtype=torch.int32)
        self.mamba_cache_indices = all_pool_idx

        cow_src_list = []
        cow_dst_indices = []
        clear_indices = []
        for i, req in enumerate(reqs):
            if req.mamba_cow_src_index is not None:
                cow_src_list.append(req.mamba_cow_src_index)
                cow_dst_indices.append(i)
                req.mamba_cow_src_index = None
                req.mamba_needs_clear = False
            elif req.mamba_needs_clear:
                clear_indices.append(i)
                req.mamba_needs_clear = False

        if cow_src_list:
            # Concatenate source tensors and extract destination indices from the device tensor
            self.mamba_cow_src_indices = torch.cat(cow_src_list).flatten().to(dtype=torch.int64)
            self.mamba_cow_dst_indices = all_pool_idx[cow_dst_indices].to(dtype=torch.int64)
        else:
            self.mamba_cow_src_indices = None
            self.mamba_cow_dst_indices = None

        if clear_indices:
            self.mamba_clear_indices = all_pool_idx[clear_indices].to(dtype=torch.int64)
        else:
            self.mamba_clear_indices = None
```

<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.io to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

<!-- Describe the purpose and goals of this pull request. -->

In PD disaggregation scenarios, NPUMLATokenToKVPool.get_state_buf_infos() unconditionally accesses self.index_k_buffer , which is initialized to None when the model has no index head (i.e., index_head_dim is None ), causing TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable .

This issue was triggered by commit d7f4761a ([PD] Refactor hybrid state transfer), which added NPUMLATokenToKVPool to the isinstance check in setup_state_kv_args , causing get_state_buf_infos() to be invoked and exposing this previously uncovered null path.

The sibling methods get_contiguous_buf_infos and get_kv_size_bytes already guard with if self.index_head_dim is not None , but get_state_buf_infos was missing this check.

```
def _chunk_copy_npu_to_cpu(self, layers, indices):
```

defects.pr_body:
<!-- Thank you for your contribution! Please follow these guidelines to enhance your pull request. If anything is unclear, submit your PR and reach out to maintainers for assistance. Join our Slack community at https://slack.sglang.io to discuss further. -->

## Motivation

<!-- Describe the purpose and goals of this pull request. -->

Commit abe2ec2af introduced a direct assignment in `DeepseekV2ForCausalLM.__init__` that overwrites `quant_config.packed_modules_mapping`. Before the overwrite, the dict (pre-populated by the weight loader for ModelSlim) has a nested structure:

```python
{
    "visual": {...},
    "vision_model": {...},
    "model": {"fused_qkv_a_proj_with_mqa": ["q_a_proj", "kv_a_proj_with_mqa"], ...},
}
```

After the overwrite, it is replaced with the model's flat dict:

```python
{"fused_qkv_a_proj_with_mqa": ["q_a_proj", "kv_a_proj_with_mqa"]}
```

ModelSlim accesses this dict via `self.packed_modules_mapping.get("model", {})`, which now returns `{}`, causing `get_linear_scheme` to return `None` and triggering `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'create_weights'`.

## Modifications

Replace direct assignment ( = ) with `.update()` when using modelslim.

reviews.body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Hardcoding `is_neox_style=False` in the shared `LlamaAttention` class is a regression for other models. While ERNIE-4.5 might use GPT-J style RoPE (`is_neox_style=False`), the Llama family of models uses NeoX style (`is_neox_style=True`). This change will likely break RoPE calculations for Llama and other NeoX-style models when running on NPU. Please pass the correct style dynamically from the rotary embedding object.

```suggestion
            is_neox_style=self.rotary_emb.is_neox_style,
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This dimension check and conditional `unsqueeze(0)` are redundant. PyTorch's broadcasting rules automatically handle the addition of a 1D tensor of shape `(E,)` or a 2D tensor of shape `(1, E)` to a 2D tensor of shape `(N, E)`. Since you have correctly removed the unconditional `.unsqueeze(0)` at line 573, these lines can be safely removed to simplify the code.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Please follow PEP 8 style guidelines by adding spaces around the assignment operator.

```suggestion
_is_npu = is_npu()
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

While this fix addresses the dimension error for ERNIE-4.5 on NPU, introducing hardware-specific logic directly into the model definition is generally discouraged as it affects maintainability. Ideally, such dimension adjustments should be encapsulated within the `TopK` layer or the NPU-specific backend implementation (e.g., in `fused_topk_npu`). This would ensure that all MoE models benefit from the fix without needing hardware-specific checks in their definitions. 

Additionally, please add spaces around the `=` operators to adhere to PEP 8.

```suggestion
        correction_bias = self.gate.e_score_correction_bias
        if _is_npu:
            correction_bias = correction_bias.squeeze(0)
```
why change this file, ernie4 model will not execute to native.
add comment, why do this

defects.root_cause: ERNIE-4.5 bias tensor has 2D shape but the NPU backend requires 1D bias, causing a shape mismatch error
defects.rationale: Shape mismatch crash. A boundary test loading ERNIE-4.5 on NPU and checking bias shapes would catch it.

![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

The `ExitStack` is not used as a context manager (via a `with` statement), which means if an exception occurs during `self.model(...)`, the environment variable overrides will not be reverted. This can lead to incorrect state leakage affecting subsequent requests in the same process. Additionally, use the pre-computed `_is_npu` variable instead of calling `is_npu()` in the forward pass for consistency and performance.

```suggestion
        with ExitStack() as exit_stack:
            if (
                _is_npu
                and self.quant_config is None
                and get_global_server_args().quantization is not None
            ):
                # ascend mtp unquant
                exit_stack.enter_context(envs.SGLANG_DEEPEP_BF16_DISPATCH.override(True))
                exit_stack.enter_context(
                    envs.DEEP_NORMAL_MODE_USE_INT8_QUANT.override(False)
                )

            hidden_states = self.model(input_ids, positions, forward_batch)
```
